OpenAI.notes: OpenAI docs render marketing headers and feedback widgets that must not gate a material change.

Anthropic.notes: Anthropic platform pages carry navigation and feedback chrome. Compliance surfaces (authentication, data-retention, access-transparency) are material and never treated as counters.

Gemini.notes: ai.google.dev appends feedback prompts and localisation banners.

Hermes.notes: GitHub release pages surface star/fork/watch counters and Assets counts that are pure noise; the release tag/title/body is material.

Apify.notes: Apify changelog and store pages embed live usage counters and social-proof lines.

Firecrawl.notes: Firecrawl GitHub release pages carry the same GitHub counter chrome as Hermes.

Exa.notes: Exa docs render a feedback widget beneath reference content.

anthropic.platform.access-transparency.label: Anthropic API compliance — Access Transparency
excludes: Claude mobile and desktop application usage
anthropic.platform.access-transparency.note: Access Transparency is a human-access audit facility covering specific Anthropic API customer-data surfaces only. It is NOT Claude Max usage telemetry and does not cover the excluded consumer/application surfaces. Changes here classify strictly under the API-compliance surface and must never be reported as Max or consumer usage telemetry.
